title: draba deserticola
intro: Draba deserticola, also known as desert whitlow-grass, is a small, annual plant native to the southwestern United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Draba deserticola is often prepared for herbal use by drying the leaves and flowers, and can be consumed as a tea or used topically in salves and ointments.
